steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This is an Inferences question asking what most logically completes the text. The passage sets up a cause-and-effect structure: captive animals showed right-handedness while wild ones did not, and the passage explicitly names the distinguishing factor — captive animals spend significantly more time with humans. The blank must follow logically from that stated cause.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — the apparent difference between the two studies' results may be partly attributable to the 1976 study using a different standard to determine handedness than the 1999 study did.: The passage never mentions any difference in methodology between the two studies. This introduces an idea not in the text. ✗
  • Choice B — the right-handedness exhibited by the captive chimpanzees and catarrhine monkeys might be, in part, an effect of extended contact with humans.: The passage points directly to captive animals spending more time with humans as the relevant distinction. This statement draws the logical inference that human contact may have caused the right-handedness. ✓
  • Choice C — the number of individuals in the study of captive chimpanzees and catarrhine monkeys is likely too small to support the claim that the species tends toward right handedness.: The passage mentions nothing about sample size. ✗
  • Choice D — the number of individuals in the study of wild chimpanzees is probably too small to support the claim that no tendency toward right-handedness was found in that population.: Again, sample size is never mentioned in the passage. ✗
B is correct.
